reviews.review_title: Played nearly every day since launch
reviews.review_description:
I personally love games where I can jump in and immediately be in the thick of the action. One of the great things about this game is that the maps change every time you play, so there’s no set pattern to follow as you progress through the levels.

The simplicity of the action and goals is also what makes this a game you can play hundreds of times without losing interest. I typically jump into VR during my lunch break, and even with all the games I have, I still choose this one nearly every time.

The look of the game is unique, and it doesn’t drain my battery with heavy rendering. The physics are awesome, but one thing I especially love is the sound effects—particularly the glass breaking when you shoot a window, lamp, bottle, or wall clock.

Even when I’m having a horrible day, knowing I can jump into this game, escape for a bit, and wreak havoc is almost therapeutic.

P.S. that said, I'd love another level or two. Or a new game from these developers.

It's good. VERY good in fact. If you like classic retro style games like old doom or Wolfernstein, you will like this. The guns feel good to use(mostly), the maps and enemies are well designed, and the bosses feel very much challenging but satisfying to beat. However..
It's not perfect, let it be known, this game is easy in the beginning, but it ramps up FAST, and i mean VERY fast on the the other difficulties, and it only gets worse with modifiers. And while MOST of the time its manageable, some of the modifiers on the harder difficulties seemingly make you rely on RNG for weapon, enemy, and ammo spawns rather than your skill, because some of these enemies are TANKY without certain weapons, to the point where multiple times I had to abuse an exploit where certain enemies that only walk in a straight line at you, won't turn corners and will get stuck, and had to sit with 1 of the basic weapons shooting around a corner for what felt like a few minutes just to take down ONE enemy. 
That being said most of the issues seem to be more focused on the modifiers rather than the base game itself.
In conclusion, it's great. Really fun, and hope these guys make a sequel or at the very least something similar.
(P.S. The compound bow modifier is the best)

I really like this game!!!!! super fun and packed with a good amount of content, but for some reason when I closed the game after finishing it on hard difficulty with old schooler mutator, it did not save and I was back to an earlier save and then died,,, from what I can recall, my wifi was just fine that day and I closed the game the same way I always do, and for some reason this is the only instance of the game reverting back to an earlier save but oh well, just my luck I suppose!!!!
No updates
For several months can't move in the game in the main menu. Sent bug reports - no luck. It was my favorite game :(

There is almost nothing I would change about this game. The combat is fun, the gun variety makes every run interesting, the beautiful low poly visuals are perfect, and the ability to break things in the environment is so satisfying. One of my favorite things about the game is just how unexpectedly hilarious it is with the silly voice lines from enemies and bosses and the funny signs on the walls like “Join the Corps or be a corpse”. The only thing I would change about this game is just adding slightly more content. This game is almost infinitely replayable and never seems to get boring but I just wish the devs were a bit more active.
This game is so fun!

I can't stop playing, this game is too good.
If the NES was a VR headset this is what we would have gotten. It's Doom meets Zelda with mega Man graphics in VR, it's incredible, buy it right now.

Great game, but currently I am experiecing a bug witch does not let me use regular locomotion, teleport movement works fine, but when I turn on regular locomotion, it doesn't move. Any help please? ( I already tried deleting and downloading it again )
Tbh this is the only rogue like i actually enjoy playing maybe its only because of the guns but who knows i still really like it

I’m almost there to beating medium difficulty, 61% completion of trophies and unlocked all weapons in just a month (I think) this game is great. Lots of challenges now I don’t know what to choose. Compound, gorilla tag crowbar climber, animal company, or Skelly?

The best Vr shooter I have played in a while, I love the art style and the unique gameplay it has, very fun game, just know if your gonna buy this game, when you die you go back to the first level lol.
Awesome rogue like shooter

reviews.review_title: Massive fun and my go-to VR game
reviews.review_description: Love the old school aesthetic! My default 'I wanna jump into VR' game - a must for any FPS fan!

Absolutely love this game. I grew up playing wolfenstein 3D and the original doom games. Now I get to be the Guy. Super intuitive and wickedly repayable (countless resets for taking stupid shots to the face that I totally.. definitely.. might have dodged) 

Only issue for me is atleast 3 or 4 time each time I play, instead of going back to the hub I get dropped outside the building on the street watching cars go by and have to relaunch the game
Very good game would recommend

reviews.review_title: Like jumping into your retro game
reviews.review_description: Standing in the starting lobby and looking out into the virtual world. The colors pop, the retro style works really well in VR. Satisfying run and gun/bullet hell-ish type rougelike. I don't think we could ever get Doom in VR but this gets pretty close.

Compound is super fun. One of the most enjoyable computer games I've ever played on any platform. I love the replay-ability, all the different modifiers, the variety of guns and enemies, the randomized level layouts.

Hats off to the creator! Thank you for the money hundreds of hours of fun.

Scratches the itch of wanting to be a one man army while still being challenging (especially in hard mode).
Great diversity in weapons and needing to conserve ammo when playing really adds to the experience. Couldn’t say anything bad about this game 10/10!
game needs a level editor, hasn't been updated in a while
I lost m progres for no reason why?
The only thing it’s missing is some old-school multiplayer (Deathmatch, TDM, CTF, etc.). Other than that, it’s perfect.

I play this pretty much every time I hop on VR. It's a very light game where I can pretty much get into it right away and just play a round or two, seeing how far I can get with the one-hit-kill mutator (which is BRUTAL, btw).

You'll also be moving a lot more than you'd think. Bullets move at a forgiving speed, but still fast enough to pose a threat. Since every enemy aims at your head, you'll find yourself moving your head around to dodge bullets with fair and accurate hitboxes. Seriously, dodging bullets and firing at your attacker is some awesome John Wick-style stuff, and I love it a lot.

Overall, easy on battery, performance is phenomenal, graphics are charming, the gameplay is very polished and fun, and easy to pick up and play at any time. It's also only a few 100 MBS so takes up very little storage space. Definitely recommended.

Being able to run around with just one other person would be great. That's the one thing I wish we could see. Oh, maybe some more guns and stages and enemy types. None of that reduces the score because that's what I want and not what's promised.

skmgreat game, every level and enemies as well as the weapon are randomized every time. its a solid buy. great game to just pick up and play for 5 mins or oan hour. still very impressed with this game even though its pretty simple, unlimited replay value, one of the best if not the best game ive bought

for the devs: would be cool t to see a game with the level randomizer an escape from an alien prison
Really, 5hrs a week for 3 weeks and motion sickness is gone. And not only for VR, real-life boat trips are now a joy.

Want a good VR shooter? This is it. Looks great, runs great, with a wide variety of weapons and mutators to keep the gameplay fresh run after run. A pinch on the hard side, but that adds to the charm. What's not to love?

There are no seated options, if you sit you are too short in the game. The opening options confused me, unable to walk forward the block is in the way so I couldn't start the game, I realised this is because I was playing seated, when I stood up I was able to select the done option. 

The reload mechanics you can't get used to very quickly.

It is like being in an 80s arcade game. The guns are fun and the aim is good, the more you play the more fun you will have.

This game is very cool, but there is no seated gameplay. In  the description, under comfort, seated is listed which is incorrect. There no setting in menu related to height or seated/standing.

Game has received zero updates or changes.

reviews.review_title: Enter the Gungeon vr and ultrakill
reviews.review_description: Felt and looked like ultrakill had a baby with enter the Gungeon and produced this so awesome.👏

reviews.review_title: One of the Best VR Shooters I've Played!
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun, and you can change the gameplay a tiny bit with mutators! This game kind of reminds of the classic Doom games but in VR! There's tons of fun weapons too!
I think this game is amazing, it's doing everything I want from a VR game. So much fun.

Where do I begin here? Compound is just absolute fun. If you've seen footage of the game and you like the retro style and the concept of the game, you'll absolutely love this game.  I'm going to highlight some of the main things that make this game so much fun:

-Weapons- There are lots of different weapons, all balanced just right for what their purpose is, and color coded so you have a nice easy visual representation of what type of weapon you have. Reloading is carefully designed for each weapon, even just reloading is fun.

-Customization: The game is highly customizable with all of the different mutators and 3 different difficulty modes, you can tweak it to whatever your comfort zone is.

-Visuals: The visuals are so bright and vibrant, and crisp. The game runs silky smooth, and the textures are all interesting. You really feel like you're in an old MS-DOS first person shooter game. The color coding also applies to enemies, the enemies will be using whatever weapon matches the color of their outfits.

-Sound & Music: Firing the weapons have big, full chunky sounding synth pulses that sound great. I find myself just firing the guns sometimes just because it feels so good. The spacial sound is great, and foot steps give you a heads up of when enemies are coming around corners. The soundtrack is killer, the synthwave music sounds so good and nails the atmosphere of the game.

Compound is the pinnacle of replayability. It's so easy to jump in and jump out of a game for short bursts, or forget your wife is waiting in the car to go to dinner. If you still love playing older DOS FPS games like Wolfenstein and love the idea of a VR equivalent, this is it. Plus being a roguelike, you're always going to get a fresh run.

Must have!

reviews.review_title: Fantastic phase of owning a VR headset
reviews.review_description:
This game is so simple yet so great. The retro style is so awesome and the variety of weapons feels really great—right when i start thinking "i wish there was a new weapon, i'm getting used to these ones", a new weapon becomes available to appear in runs! The weapons are all so fun and unique and each have their own good uses.

I would suggest playing the game on medium difficulty for a while, but then as soon as you're able to beat a whole run bump it up to hard difficulty, aside from the expected player and enemy health differences, enemies will shoot more aggressively and enemy shots travel faster which feels more realistic and makes you more cautious about peering around corners.

Once you start looking into achievement-hunting i'd suggest you start playing modifier runs while working away at the "beat the game on the [difficulty] difficulty level" achievements. I think that even though they try to stay balanced, the modifier runs are always easier than regular runs (but the gimmicks are so fun) so i personally ran them all on the very hard difficulty.

Binged the hell out of this game for a couple months and loved it to bits.
This game is like doom 1991 and it’s really enjoyable to play. I recommend playing it!

This is, simply put, a phenomenal game. It's incredibly fun, and with enough unlocks to keep you busy for a long time. 

I know some people might not like the low-fi aesthetic, but it works really well. Load times are near-nonexistent and everything runs incredibly smoothly. Also, don't think that the low-fi choices mean a lack of attention to detail- the devs clearly poured a lot of love into this game, and everything is really well done. The sound design is great, the guns are varied and all fun, the enemies span a nice range, and there are lots of unlockables. And just to say it, I personally absolutely *love* the look they went with. Stellar job all around.

It's just an overall great game, and definitely deserves a spot in your library.

This game is so fun,doomenstein meets they live,Zone two got me laughing. The necronomicon and obey mags,the picture of nada as well,the cult freak from blood  For real you brought back my childhood in a very fun way. Nice Easter eggs 💕
Funi VR roguelite gaem, is gud, you get, you plae, or you die

The store listing says this game supported seated play. As far as I can tell, there isn’t an option to change player height. The result is that you’re too low when playing seated. I’m either missing something or the listing needs to change.
Game is cool at first then becomes a massive repetitive slog to get 100% and unlock Minigun.

Oldschool style FPS shooter. Empressive gameplay, mostly destructible environments and ragdoll physics...
But if you want to play real superior old school shooter, just play original Doom 2 and Wolfenstein in vr with the Brutal mod! This is in 100 time better than Compound!

The shotgun bullets are so spread out and so quick, to the point if I even tried to fight 1 on 1 on normal, I just die. I have to hug a wall and blind fire to win, and this just feels really unprofessional as far as game design goes. I can only get so far before having to restart, because of the unbalanced enemies.
This game is amazing, it captures the look and feel of Doom in vr. The physics are amazing, the guns are amazing, everything’s amazing. I would definitely recommend buying this game.

reviews.review_title: If you get motion sickness, AVOID
reviews.review_description: You can't reduce movement speed, you can't add a vignette when you move, you can't even use locomotion with teleport at the same time and there's no dash for back-stepping or dodging enemy attacks. I feel sick if I play this for more than 10 minutes, so I couldn't comment on the gameplay. Seriously, the quality of life features are so limited, it seems like it's not even a VR game, but something that was made for PC and then ported to VR.

God forbid you need to take a break from the game and come back later because you will lose your progress and lose your guns. I was in a nice mall level had to take a break and when I come back I'm in the sewer. Both guns I like, gone. Just gone. And they replaced the best gun in the game imo the first pistol you get with a cheesey spread pistol you can't aim and has no range. That SUCKS!!!

Bro it's 2024 I spent money on this and you can't even save after every level? You give us good guns in the beginning and bad guns in the middle? Lame. The first pistol needs to remain in every level or you need to fix your saves so I don't lose my good guns because I took a freaking break after beating a level.
The store page says that sitting is supported. This is a lie. This game is too intense for me to play standing and idk or care how to cheese sitting mode just for this game.

reviews.review_title: $10 game at best, $4 game at worse
reviews.review_description: Not sure I'd qualify this as a game, feels more like a demo or someone's school project.  The art style is fun, but somehow feels more limited then the games it tries to emulate.   I know it's a rogue like game, but even so the repetition is daunting.   I feel like with at least good rogue likes you eventually get unlocks that allow you to progress through the first few level faster, but not here.  I don't know how people have the time and patience to drag themselves through this over and over, but I guess if you have the time to waste then you might like this.  However, even then I wouldn't pay more then $10 for this given the limited playability.

Compound is just a great game, the old school style graphics design is spot on and it fits the rouge like game perfectly, tons on replayability and new weapons and achievements, 

In your apartment where u start you have a trophy stand where you can check achievement progress and you also have a chain gun placed in a space in the room however that is tied to gettin gold on all achievements, each achievement comes in 3s, broze, silver and gold, 

However this is where im sure alot of people will get annoyed, because gettin all gold achievements will take a ridiculous amount of time and skill that very very very few humans are actually capable of, im a very skilled gamer if i do say so myself yet some achievements seem utterly impossible 

I've lost count of how many hours I've put into the game, easily hundreds, so they dangle the carrot (chaingun) infront of you from the moment you load in yet less then 5% of the playerbase will ever unlock it, while i understand stress, effort, perseverance etc is required for such a reward, there is such a thing as being too unrealistic, the chaingun feels more like a cruel joke by the devs then a realistic reward,,, 

But thats the Only downside to this game, however that reason alone drops this review to only 3 stars yes im that fed up about it,
Make easy mode a bit better and more easy when Playing with damage amplifier

There's another game that tries to use old school shooter graphics and gameplay in VR, but it failed for one big reason: flat sprites (character models). Games like doom used sprites to great effect, but in VR it looks like you're fighting cardboard cutouts. Ruins the immersion. I refunded that game. I think I'll keep this one.

I have yet to finish a loop, but after a couple hours I'm impressed. The 3D models make the old school graphics work amazingly well in VR. Added bonus: the simple graphics allow for some complex explosions with shrapnel flying around and enemies hitting walls and knocking down signs.

This is a fun and satisfying roguelike that provides just enough incentive to keep playing the loops. Difficulty seems to be ramping up pretty well, although mostly it's just more bad guys in each room. Hopefully there's enough variety down the line to keep us coming back... 

Things that need work: controls are weird and no holsters. I have a nerve condition and cannot squeeze triggers for the entirety of a game. I wish there was an accessibility option to allow for toggle triggers! There is a workaround using the "store weapon" button, but it's not very intuitive and easy to lose guns while playing.

On that note, the process of stowing weapons and reloading guns also goes against what most VR titles do. I'm all for originality, but when your main focus is shooting, please let us use our muscle memory and use "grab" (2nd trigger) for reloading bullets (not the primary trigger) and use holsters on our body to switch guns.

reviews.review_title: A great game held back by its subgenre
reviews.review_description:
First of all, there is quite a lot Compound got right for me as a throwback FPS. The aesthetic is fantastic, the guns are fun to use and as far as actually being "inside" something like the original Doom is concerned...this game nails it. I also appreciate being able to move with the analog stick versus the awful teleportation mechanic found in games like Doom VFR.

However, its roguelike nature truly holds back its potential in my opinion. In the classic first person shooters, part of the appeal was that the maps were actually designed by somebody and rife with secrets. While this game is a blast for the first couple of hours, it doesn't take long for repetition to set in as you'll be seeing the same enemy types and the same map layouts over and over since the last boss is insanely hard (not that this would be a problem in a game that doesn't have permadeath). 

On top of the maps being spat out by a machine rather than actual talent, Compound also lacks the inclusion of old school style secrets and it's a shame. Part of the fun in many classic FPS games was finding a misaligned texture on the wall or perhaps hitting a switch somewhere that triggered a door elsewhere on the map and subsequently being rewarded for figuring these things out. You don't get that here. 

As harsh as I'm being, I still do like the concept of this game and I'm glad I supported it. The framework is here and I think they could really knock it out of the park with a sequel that has hand crafted levels and secrets.  I understand smaller devs like to use the roguelike model to cut costs...but what we have here is a concept that is 100% worth more than what this model could ever offer. Even if Compound 2 cost a bit extra...I'd gladly pay up for it if rogueness/permadeath was taken out of the equation.

Just started playing. So far it's awesome. Unless I'm missing something, it drives me nuts having to start over after getting killed just once. If the theme is supposed to feel retro, why not at least have 3 lives before having to start over. Am I missing something here?

reviews.review_title: Could be great, but it’s not
reviews.review_description:
Let me start off by saying I enjoy the game, the art style is really nice, and it looks really crisp on the Quest Pro.

However, there are issues which stop me giving it a higher score:
- procedural level layouts are boring. No verticality or anything drastic that mixes up the gameplay. If dev added some verticality, maybe some levels with different mechanics (such as no lights and needing a torch? Or more hazards you need to avoid? I don’t know, but compared to InDeath’s layouts these are pretty uninteresting).
- no exploration on the levels. It would be better to hide secrets around the levels, maybe have the (up) elevator in a different location you need to find (like how Doom has you searching for the exit). 
- some guns are way too big and block most of the view. The rifle scope is pointless and just gets in the way. 
- reloading is a chore. It’s too inconsistent and fiddly, having to ensure the angle is near perfect as well as the position for ammo. It’s more fiddly than realistic reloading, which I actually enjoy.
- hands are completely misaligned (not sure if this is a Quest pro issue. I can test on my Quest 2 later to see). This might be aiding the reloading issue.

It does have some good points, but it’s held back by the issues above. Being as the game is all about procedural levels, and them being boring in layout, it’s hard to rate it higher.

The art style seems like doom. Great, low res means more room for expansive levels and better game play right? Nope. 
Walk in shoot, leave the same way you came in. 
Progression? You die start on lv.1 
Rinse wash repeat. 
Crazy thought here, how about you make a Doom clone that is like Doom.
I want to like this game more but it feels like a nostalgic cash grab that leaves me thinking I've been suckered.

I admit it... I guess I just don't understand Roguelite.  I don't understand why, after going through a few really easy levels, I'm suddenly facing a whole horde of bad guys and I've got no place to hide.  I don't understand why, after getting killed by said bad guys, I have to start over but against MORE bad guys than the first time through.  If Rougelite = GRIND, GRIND, GRIND, then that = NO THANKS to me.

I'm not an FPS kind of gamer, but this reminded me of Wolfenstein and Doom, so I picked it up. I can't deny that it is a fun shooter if you're into retro and not into zombies. However, I deducted a star from a 4-star review because, unlike what the description says, there is no sitting mode. If you're older or have a bad back, wait until it's updated with a sitting mode. And, since this is a rogue-lite, you can't save your progress. If you need to take a break and sit down, you're SOL.

There is not any menu that would allow you to access controller settings. It lets you choose settings when u start the game for 1st time - but after that no possibility to change that. Very disappointed because of that

developer_response.body: Hi. You can change any and all of the settings at any time with the green button on the back of either wrist

reviews.review_description: This game is so freaking good, and its made by a single dev, hats off to you Bevan!

I have really enjoyed my time with this game, the enemies are fun, guns are even better, and there is just enough variability to keep it interesting. If you are on the fence, drop the cash and pick this gem up.
This game is something special. My only concern in the unclear instructions on how to reload your gun
